text: Paintball Act 2018. Use this form to apply for a new international paintball competitor permit. An international paintball competitor permit authorises an individual to possess, other than at an authorised paintball venue, 1 (one) or more paintball markers that the person bought into New South Wales.
Am I eligible? You must be at least 18 years of age, be a fit and proper person, and reside outside of Australia.

Important information: You need a permit to bring a paintball marker into New South Wales from overseas. An international paintball competitor permit is only valid for 1 (one) year. Paintball permits do not allow you to use a paintball marker outside an authorised venue.

How to lodge: Complete this form and email it to FTlicensing@customerservice.nsw.gov.au. You must make payment online before submitting your application. Please record the receipt number below before lodging — your application will take longer to assess if you do not provide this information.
IMPORTANT — Are your details correct? NSW Fair Trading must be notified within 14 days of any changes occurring to the permit holder's name, address, or other registered particulars.

label: International Residential Address — House Number and Street Address (not PO Box)
Email Address (all correspondence will be sent to this address)
Competition 1 — Email Address of Competition Organiser
Competition 2 — Email Address of Competition Organiser

text: If you answer Yes to any of the below questions, you must complete the Paintball Permit Additional Details form and submit it as an attachment to this application.

label: Have you been convicted of a relevant offence in the last 10 years?

text: Note: a relevant offence means an offence against any of the following Acts or Regulations under those Acts: Paintball Act 2018, Firearms Act 1996, Crimes Act 1990 (but only in respect of an offence involving a firearm or offensive weapon or instrument), Weapons Prohibition Act 1998, Fair Act 1987 or Australian Consumer Law (NSW).

label: Have you had an equivalent authorisation suspended or cancelled (other than on your request) under the law of another Australian jurisdiction?

text: Note: An equivalent authorisation includes the following licences issued in respect of a paintball marker: (a) an adult firearms licence issued in the category of paintball marker under the Firearms Act 1996 of the ACT; (b) a licence for a category A weapon within the meaning of the Weapons Act 1990 of QLD; (c) a licence for a category A weapon within the meaning of the Firearms Act 2015 of South Australia; (d) a category A firearms licence within the meaning of the Firearms Act 1996 of Tasmania; (e) a paintball marker licence within the meaning of the Firearms Act 1996 of Victoria; (f) a licence for a subcategory E5 firearm issued under the Firearms Act 1973 of Western Australia.

label: Have you been disqualified (other than on grounds of not residing in the relevant jurisdiction) from holding an equivalent authorisation under the law of another Australian jurisdiction?
Do you have any mental incapacity that would impact your ability to hold a permit in NSW?
Are you an involuntary, forensic or correctional patient within the meaning of the Mental Health Act 2007 (NSW)?
Are you a protected person within the meaning of the NSW Trustee and Guardian Act 2009 (NSW)?

text:
I certify that the particulars specified in this application and all attachments are, to the best of my knowledge, true and correct in every detail.

Statement under the Privacy and Personal Information Protection Act 1998. The applicant for this permit: (1) authorises NSW Fair Trading to make any inquiries and to receive and disclose any information which is relevant to the applicant's initial and ongoing eligibility to hold a permit under the Paintball Act 2018; (2) accepts that failure to supply information required on this application form may delay the processing of the application; and (3) has a right to seek access to and correction of information supplied.

Making a false or misleading statement, giving false or misleading information, or producing false or misleading documents is a serious offence which may render you liable to prosecution for offences including under the Crimes Act 1900 which may result in penalties including a fine or imprisonment.

Privacy Collection Notice. Your information is being collected by the Department of Customer Service on behalf of the Commissioner for NSW Fair Trading, in accordance with the Privacy and Personal Information Protection Act 1998 (PPIP Act) and Health Records and Information Privacy Act 2002 (HRIP Act). NSW Fair Trading collects your personal information for the purpose of assessing your application for a paintball permit under the Paintball Act 2018 and the Paintball Regulation 2024. We may collect your full name, contact information, details of previous permits, third party details, proof of identity documents, details of relevant offences and pending charges in the last ten years, financial information including bankruptcy, and details of mental capacity where applicable. Your information is provided voluntarily however if you do not provide it, we may not be able to process your application. We may share your information with NSW Government agencies (including the NSW Police Force), other States, Territories and/or the Commonwealth for compliance purposes, and with Service NSW where this form is lodged at a Service NSW Service Centre. You have the right to request access to and correction of your personal information. For privacy enquiries or complaints contact FTPrivacy@customerservice.nsw.gov.au.
